Track record
Exposure rose sharply last year.
The desk cut the biggest positions in March.
The trend has improved since March.
Breaches have dropped to zero this year.
Sergio, thanks for joining. Before the committee, let me ask the big one: how has the risk profile on the desk changed this year?
In short, it has improved a lot. Last year was hard: exposure climbed to a new peak in the first quarter. But since March, the trend has been much better.
Good. And the limit breaches? Last year we had several.
They have dropped to zero this year. We fixed the controls in the spring, and so far we have kept a clean track record with the regulator.
That is a strong story. Was there any bad month, or has it been steady all the way?
There was one dip in April, but the trend rebounded fast. Since then it has been steady, and the buffer has held.
Last year, exposure climbed to a new peak, but it has fallen since January.
The trend has improved steadily over the past six months.
We cut the biggest positions in the first quarter.
So far, we have kept a strong track record with the regulator.
The market dipped sharply in March, but it has been steady since then.

Hello, Sergio. This is Marina, with a short review of how the desk's risk profile has changed this year, before the committee meeting. Last year was hard. In the first quarter, exposure climbed sharply to a new peak after a market shock, and volatility rose fast. Then the desk cut the biggest positions in the spring. Since March, the picture has improved month by month. The number of limit breaches has dropped to zero this year, and we have kept a clean track record with the regulator. The trend has been steady for six months now. One thing has not changed yet: the credit desk has not sent the final figures, so please chase them before Friday. Thank you.

Hi Sergio, it is Paulo from the credit desk. A quick note before your review. I looked at the numbers again this morning. Last year our provisions rose a lot, but they have come down every quarter since January, which is good news for the committee. The stress-test results have improved as well, and the buffer has been stable for months. I did notice one dip in April, but the trend recovered fast after that. So far, no new deficiency has appeared this year. I have already updated the slides, so you have the latest figures. Can you call me after the meeting? Thanks.

Last year, exposure climbed sharply to a new peak. In the first quarter, the desk cut the biggest positions. Since March, the risk profile has improved month by month. The number of limit breaches has dropped to zero this year. So far, the committee has approved every plan on time.
